behaviors.description: An image containing explicit content is flagged and held for review, not published.
behaviors.justification: Failure boundary is the vision model’s real classification judgment (LLM-DEP yes). A stubbed classifier can only confirm routing/hold logic and would miss misclassification of explicit images (MOCK-HIDE yes). Not Workflow because this behavior is driven by a single model decision plus deterministic hold/publish logic.
behaviors.test_description: Submit a curated set of truly explicit images through the real moderation endpoint using the production vision model path, then assert each is marked flagged and routed to human-review hold state, with no publish event.

behaviors.description: A text post containing hate speech is flagged regardless of obfuscation techniques (e.g., letter substitution, unicode lookalikes).
behaviors.justification: This is model robustness/quality on obfuscated hate speech (LLM-DEP yes). Deterministic fixtures or mocked outputs will always pass and cannot validate real model detection quality (MOCK-HIDE yes). Not Workflow: one model judgment is the core failure boundary.
behaviors.test_description: Run a corpus of obfuscated hate-speech text samples against the real text moderation model path and assert they are flagged and held for review across variants (leet substitutions, homoglyphs, spacing/punctuation tricks).

behaviors.description: A post that passes both content checks is published rather than held or silently dropped.
behaviors.justification: Primary risk is module handoff/orchestration: image result + text result + decision/publish state transition (MOCK-MISS yes at module boundary). Real model quality is not under test because behavior text says the post already passes both checks (LLM-DEP no for this behavior). Real OS/network/subprocess is not required to expose silent-drop/hold-vs-publish logic errors.
behaviors.test_description: Wire moderation service, decision engine, and persistence/event publisher with in-process fakes for model responses returning PASS for both checks; assert final persisted state is published, no hold record exists, and a publish event is emitted exactly once.
behaviors.plan_consistent_note: System rejected: behavior does not require real network/OS effects; Agentic rejected: correctness does not depend on model output quality, only on PASS/PASS handoff and decisioning.

behaviors.description: The full moderation pipeline — image check, text check, publish decision — completes within 5 seconds of submission.
behaviors.justification: This is end-to-end SLA across multiple agentic steps in sequence (vision call + text model call + final decision), so MULTI-STEP yes and LLM/API path must be real enough to expose latency composition. Lower levels with stubs can hide real queueing/network/model latency and miss true 5s regressions (MOCK-HIDE yes).
behaviors.test_description: Execute an end-to-end performance scenario with real moderation model calls for both image and text on representative mixed posts, measure submission-to-final-decision latency, and assert p95 (or required percentile) is <= 5 seconds under peak-hour concurrency profile.

behaviors.description: When the AI vision model is unavailable, image posts are queued for retry rather than auto-approved.
behaviors.justification: Behavior targets fallback orchestration on dependency failure (queue-for-retry vs auto-approve), which can be triggered by an in-process model client fake returning unavailable errors. Model output quality is irrelevant (LLM-DEP no), and behavior text does not require real network timeout semantics specifically.
behaviors.test_description: With service modules wired together and a vision-client stub that returns unavailable/error, submit image posts and assert they enter retry queue with correct metadata/backoff state and are never marked approved/published.
behaviors.plan_consistent_note: System considered but rejected because behavior only specifies unavailable outcome, not OS/network-specific failure modes that require real infra to reproduce.

behaviors.description: The moderation decision for a borderline image is consistent when the same image is submitted multiple times within a short window.
behaviors.justification: Consistency risk depends on real model variability for borderline content (LLM-DEP yes). Mocked deterministic outputs would mask inconsistency and cannot validate whether repeated real inferences drift (MOCK-HIDE yes). Workflow considered because multiple submissions are sequenced, but this is primarily repeated single-step model judgment consistency rather than a multi-stage agent pipeline.
behaviors.test_description: Submit the same borderline image repeatedly within the target window through the real vision moderation path, collect decisions, and assert consistency criterion (e.g., identical hold/publish outcome or within defined tolerance) while verifying any dedupe/cache policy behavior.
behaviors.plan_consistent_note: Workflow rejected: repeated invocations test consistency of one model decision type, not correctness of a multi-agent stage handoff pipeline.
